meta.name: Digital Sanctuary
meta.description: Design system for a men's emotional support, legal information, and crisis resource platform.

primary._usage: 500 = Slate Blue, primary interactive color (buttons, links, focus). 800 = Deep Navy, used for headers, nav, dark surfaces, and as the anchor brand color.

secondary._usage: Emerald. Used for growth, progress, calm-positive states, completed steps. Doubles as the 'success' semantic base.

amber._usage: Warm accent for highlights, gentle encouragement, in-progress states. Doubles as the 'warning' semantic base — never used to signal danger.

neutral._usage: 0 = White. 25 = Off White (page background, light theme). 100-200 = Soft Gray (borders, dividers). 500-600 = Warm Gray (secondary text). 900 = Charcoal (primary text, dark surfaces).

semantic._usage: Error/red is reserved exclusively for true emergencies, destructive confirmations, and irreversible actions. It must never appear for routine validation or default states.

emergency._usage: Crisis banner and emergency action card only. Single, restrained use case — not a general alert color.

scale._usage: Headings use weight 600 max (never 700+) to avoid an aggressive tone. Body copy never drops below 16px for primary reading content.

radius._usage: Soft corners throughout. No sharp 0px/2px radii except rare table-cell edge cases.

spacing._usage: Strict 8pt-derived scale: 4 8 12 16 24 32 48 64. No off-scale values in product UI.

elevation._usage: 1 = resting cards. 2 = hover/raised cards. 3 = popovers/dropdowns. 4 = dialogs/drawers. Never use harder shadows than level 4.

motion._usage: Default to fade + 4-8px slide for entrances. Scale transforms stay within 0.96-1.0. All motion must degrade to opacity-only fades under prefers-reduced-motion.

breakpoints._usage: 4-column grid on mobile (gutter 16px), 8-column on tablet (gutter 24px), 12-column on desktop (gutter 24px, max-width 1280px).

iconography._usage: Rounded, minimal line icons only. No filled/duotone icon sets, no emoji as functional UI elements.
